sections.content:

Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: - Level: 1 - Board: Black background, gray border. - Objects: - Purple ring: centered around (21, 39). - Purple disk: now at (59, 27), was at (53, 22). - White star: now at (53, 22), was at (47, 17). - Gray star: now at (60, 28) or similar (pushed off the end of the original line).
- Action model: Clicking an object on the line shifts it and all objects behind it forward along the line's current orientation. - Goal: Move the purple disk to the purple ring.
- Recent findings: - Clicking the purple disk at (53, 22) moved it to the end of the line (59, 27) and shifted the white star forward to (53, 22).
- Plan: 1. The purple disk is now at the end of the line. I need to change the line's direction or move the disk off the line toward the ring. 2. Try clicking the purple disk at (59, 27) to see if it continues moving in the same direction or if it can be "launched" toward the ring. 3. Alternatively, try clicking the ring to see if it attracts the disk. Action model update: The "line" seems to be a sequence of slots. Clicking an object moves it to the next slot and shifts preceding objects forward. Click the purple disk at (59, 27).
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The user wants to move the purple disk to the purple ring.
The current state is:
- Purple Ring: (23, 38)
- Purple Disk: (40, 33)
- White Star: (21, 39)
- Gray Star: (59, 27)
Wait, the White Star is at (21, 39), which is very close to the Purple Ring (23, 38).
Looking at the image, the White Star is actually *inside* or on top of the Purple Ring.
The Purple Disk is halfway between the ring and the Gray Star.
The action model seems to be: clicking an object on the line moves it and everything "behind" it forward.
Looking at the previous transition:
- I clicked at (21, 39), which was the position of the Purple Ring (approximately).
- The result was that the White Star moved to (21, 39), the Purple Disk moved to (40, 33), and the Gray Star moved to (59, 27).
- Actually, it looks like the entire set of objects shifted.
Let's re-examine the positions before and after the click at (21, 39).
Before:
- Purple Disk: (56, 24)
- White Star: (33, 0) - wait, the previous output said White Star: (33, 0), but that's probably a bug in my summary or the segmentation.
Actually, looking at the image before the click, the objects were at the bottom left.
After the click at (21, 39), the objects are now on a line from (21, 39) to (59, 27).
The White Star is at the top end (21, 39).
The Purple Disk is in the middle (40, 33).
The Gray Star is at the bottom end (59, 27).
The goal is to get the Purple Disk to the Purple Ring (23, 38).
Currently, the White Star is at (21, 39), which is basically the ring's position.
If I click the Purple Disk at (40, 33), it should move forward to the next slot, and the White Star should move forward too? Or does the object clicked move to the front?
In the first few moves:
- Clicked disk at (47, 17) -> Disk moved to (53, 22), White star moved to (47, 17).
- Clicked disk at (53, 22) -> Disk moved to (59, 27), White star moved to (53, 22).
So clicking the disk moves it forward one slot and moves the object behind it forward one slot.
Now:
- White Star is at (21, 39) (the ring).
- Purple Disk is at (40, 33).
- Gray Star is at (59, 27).
If I click the Purple Disk at (40, 33), it should move to the next slot (21, 39) and the White Star should move further forward? Or does it wrap around?
Wait, if the line is [Ring, Slot 2, Slot 3, Slot 4, ...], and the objects are at [Ring, Slot 2, Slot 4].
Actually, the line seems to be [ (21, 39), (40, 33), (59, 27) ].
Objects are at:
- White Star: (21, 39)
- Purple Disk: (40, 33)
- Gray Star: (59, 27)
If I click the Purple Disk at (40, 33), it will move to (21, 39), and the White Star will move... where? Maybe it gets pushed off the line or moves to the next available slot?
Let's try clicking the Purple Disk at (40, 33).
